report_subtitle: Partial river-first handoff with Thurmond sediment bridge and incomplete pressure context
context_summary: This partial context is already river-first, but the next pass still needs stronger pollutant and pressure joins on the Savannah River. Hartwell, Russell, and Thurmond stay in the report as explanatory support, while Thurmond remains the sediment-study focus.
narrative_rules: Start from the Savannah River mainstem.
Pressures and pollutants on the river are part of the analytical core.
Reservoir operations explain the river signal and do not replace it.
The 20-year target must remain explicit even when coverage is shorter.

coverage_summary.rule: Treat 20 years as the analytical target horizon. If real availability falls below this, the report must show the gap by source, layer, entity, and variable.
coverage_summary.actual_summary: Current safe scope already supports river hydrology and a partial chemistry layer, but pressure and pollutant joins still need strengthening before the HTML can claim a mature river-pressure narrative.
notes: River-first framing is already mandatory in the HTML.
Pressure and pollutant layers should remain visible as partial when the EDA has not yet normalized them.
Do not present Hartwell and Russell as protagonists if the strongest evidence still sits on the mainstem and at Thurmond.

coverage_layers.actual_period: real period must be filled by the next EDA pass

metrics.label: River-first frame
metrics.note: The HTML must now start from the Savannah River.

metrics.label: Pressure layer maturity
metrics.note: Pollutant and pressure joins still need stronger structured support.

sources.sediment_master_data: analise_sedimentos/Most Corrected Master_Data Clarks Hill Lake (version 3).xlsb.xlsx

source_note.text: This partial context is grounded only in local collected artifacts and partial staging. Any stronger claim about pollutant-pressure continuity still depends on the next EDA round.

sections.title: River signal first
sections.body: The hero, top metrics, and the first figures must all keep the Savannah River as the main storyline.

sections.title: River pressures and pollutants
sections.body: Keep pollutant and pressure context visible even when it is incomplete, instead of burying it under reservoir operations.

sections.title: Coverage status against the 20-year target
sections.body: Show real coverage honestly and do not shorten the target horizon silently.

sections.title: Reservoir modulation as explanatory annex
sections.body: Use Hartwell, Russell, and Thurmond as support for the river signal.

sections.title: Bridge to the Thurmond sediment interpretation
sections.body: Keep Thurmond as the sediment target without letting it silently stand in for the full system.

current_caveats: The pressure and pollutant layer is still partial and should remain explicit in the HTML.
Do not imply system-wide parity if Hartwell and Russell are still support-only in the current evidence.
Do not imply 20-year chemistry coverage when the returned series are shorter.

interpretation_points: The Thurmond sediment notebook remains the interpretive anchor.
The HTML should use river and pressure context to explain, not replace, the sediment interpretation.
sediment_summary.caveat: Granulometric caveats and short chemistry coverage must remain visible.

figures.title: Mainstem hydrologic coverage
figures.summary: The safest current layer is still the river signal.
figures.interpretation: This should remain the first figure block.

figures.title: River pressure and pollutant context
figures.summary: Partial pressure context is already part of scope.
figures.interpretation: Keep it visible and honest until the next EDA export strengthens it.
figures.highlight: Pressure context stays partial, not hidden.
